title: diphasiastrum digitatum
intro: Diphasiastrum digitatum, also known as the finger fern or finger polypody, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Lycopodiopsida class and is native to various regions, including North America and Europe. The medicinal properties of Diphasiastrum digitatum include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Diphasiastrum digitatum can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
